mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with support for various hardware configurations. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware optimizations and potential complexity in setup for distributed environments.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the vLLM command-line interface to manage deployments.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.18.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a managed Kubernetes cluster with the required specifications.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31

```

> Adjust zone and node count as needed.
commands: gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local environment to interact with the new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --zone us-central1-a

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --zone us-central1-a

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Ensure that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the Ready state.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the vLLM application using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> Add the vLLM Helm repository.
> Install vLLM in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for the vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment vllm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --zone us-central1-a --quiet

```

> This will remove the vLLM deployment.
> This will delete the entire cluster and all associated resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --zone us-central1-a --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Backup existing configurations, upgrade the deployment, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> Backup current deployment configurations.
> Upgrade to the latest version of vLLM.
> Verify that all pods are running and healthy.
commands: kubectl get all -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize existing nodes to meet resource requirements.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for detailed error messages.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The request to the Kubernetes API server timed out.

**Fix:**
Check the network connectivity and ensure the Kubernetes API server is reachable.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new quantization methods and improved integration with Hugging Face models.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n vllm' showing all pods in the Running state.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31
gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --zone us-central1-a

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
